member.summary: Indicates that the value of the marked element could be null sometimes, so the check for null is necessary before its usage.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.NotNullAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the value of the marked element could never be null.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.ItemCanBeNullAttribute

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.StringFormatMethodAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the marked method builds string by format pattern and (optional) arguments. Parameter, which contains format string, should be given in constructor. The format string should be in -like form.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.StringFormatMethodAttribute.#ctor(System.String)
member.param: Specifies which parameter of an annotated method should be treated as format-string

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.ValueProviderAttribute
member.summary: For a parameter that is expected to be one of the limited set of values. Specify fields of which type should be used as values for this parameter.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.InvokerParameterNameAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the function argument should be string literal and match one of the parameters of the caller function. For example, ReSharper annotates the parameter of .

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.NotifyPropertyChangedInvocatorAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the method is contained in a type that implements System.ComponentModel.INotifyPropertyChanged interface and this method is used to notify that some property value changed.
member.remarks:
The method should be non-static and conform to one of the supported signatures:
NotifyChanged(string)
NotifyChanged(params string[])
NotifyChanged{T}(Expression{Func{T}})
NotifyChanged{T,U}(Expression{Func{T,U}})
SetProperty{T}(ref T, T, string)

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.ContractAnnotationAttribute
member.summary: Describes dependency between method input and output.
member.syntax:
Function Definition Table syntax:
FDT ::= FDTRow [;FDTRow]*
FDTRow ::= Input => Output | Output <= Input
Input ::= ParameterName: Value [, Input]*
Output ::= [ParameterName: Value]* {halt|stop|void|nothing|Value}
Value ::= true | false | null | notnull | canbenull
If method has single input parameter, it's name could be omitted.
Using halt (or void/nothing, which is the same) for method output means that the methos doesn't return normally (throws or terminates the process).
Value canbenull is only applicable for output parameters.
You can use multiple [ContractAnnotation] for each FDT row, or use single attribute with rows separated by semicolon. There is no notion of order rows, all rows are checked for applicability and applied per each program state tracked by R# analysis.

item.code: [ContractAnnotation("halt <= condition: false")] public void Assert(bool condition, string text) // regular assertion method
[ContractAnnotation("s:null => true")] public bool IsNullOrEmpty(string s) // string.IsNullOrEmpty()
[ContractAnnotation("=> true, result: notnull; => false, result: null")] public bool TryParse(string s, out Person result)

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.LocalizationRequiredAttribute

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.CannotApplyEqualityOperatorAttribute

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.BaseTypeRequiredAttribute

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.UsedImplicitlyAttribute

member.summary: This attribute is intended to mark publicly available API which should not be removed and so is treated as used.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.InstantHandleAttribute
member.summary: Tells code analysis engine if the parameter is completely handled when the invoked method is on stack. If the parameter is a delegate, indicates that delegate is executed while the method is executed. If the parameter is an enumerable, indicates that it is enumerated while the method is executed.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.PureAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that a method does not make any observable state changes. The same as System.Diagnostics.Contracts.PureAttribute.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.MustUseReturnValueAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the return value of method invocation must be used.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.ProvidesContextAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the type member or parameter of some type, that should be used instead of all other ways to get the value that type. This annotation is useful when you have some "context" value evaluated and stored somewhere, meaning that all other ways to get this value must be consolidated with existing one.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.PathReferenceAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that a parameter is a path to a file or a folder within a web project. Path can be relative or absolute, starting from web root (~).

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.SourceTemplateAttribute
member.summary: An extension method marked with this attribute is processed by ReSharper code completion as a 'Source Template'. When extension method is completed over some expression, it's source code is automatically expanded like a template at call site.
member.remarks: Template method body can contain valid source code and/or special comments starting with '$'. Text inside these comments is added as source code when the template is applied. Template parameters can be used either as additional method parameters or as identifiers wrapped in two '$' signs. Use the attribute to specify macros for parameters.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.MacroAttribute
member.summary: Allows specifying a macro for a parameter of a source template.
member.remarks: You can apply the attribute on the whole method or on any of its additional parameters. The macro expression is defined in the property. When applied on a method, the target template parameter is defined in the property. To apply the macro silently for the parameter, set the property value = -1.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.MacroAttribute.Expression
member.summary: Allows specifying a macro that will be executed for a source template parameter when the template is expanded.

members.member:
Allows specifying which occurrence of the target parameter becomes editable when the template is deployed.
If the target parameter is used several times in the template, only one occurrence becomes editable; other occurrences are changed synchronously. To specify the zero-based index of the editable occurrence, use values >= 0. To make the parameter non-editable when the template is expanded, use -1. >

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.JetBrains.MacroAttribute.Target
member.summary: Identifies the target parameter of a source template if the is applied on a template method.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Attributes.PluginLicenseModuleIdAttribute
member.summary: Specifies plugin's licensable module identifier.
member.remarks:
If plugin is intended to be distributed by “license per plugin” scheme, contact iiko to register licensable module for your plugin, then apply this attribute to plugin with received module identifier.
Each plugin instance will require another one license slot, so total slots number for given license module should correspond to count of simultaneously running iikoFronts with your plugin installed to.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.Attributes.HeightRatio
member.summary: Barcode Height-to-Width Ratio Defines the ratio of the barcode's height to its width. This attribute value is used to calculate the height of the barcode based on its width and the specified height-to-width ratio. Supported Values: Positive floating-point number using a period (.) as the decimal separator. Default Value: Varies depending on the model. Typically between 0.3 and 0.35.
member.remarks: For some models, if the calculated height of the barcode exceeds 255 dots, the value will be capped at 255 due to protocol limitations.

member.summary: Specifies the "L" (Low) level of error correction for a QR code as defined in ISO/IEC 18004:2015.
member.remarks: This level allows up to 7% of the codewords to be restored.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.AttributeValues.Medium
member.summary: Specifies the "M" (Medium) level of error correction for a QR code as defined in ISO/IEC 18004:2015.
member.remarks: This level allows up to 15% of the codewords to be restored.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.AttributeValues.High
member.summary: Specifies the "Q" (Quartile) level of error correction for a QR code as defined in ISO/IEC 18004:2015.
member.remarks: This level allows up to 25% of the codewords to be restored.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.AttributeValues.Ultra
member.summary: Specifies the "H" (High) level of error correction for a QR code as defined in ISO/IEC 18004:2015.
member.remarks: This level allows up to 30% of the codewords to be restored.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.AttributeValues.Fit
member.summary: Image will be resized proportionally (stretched or squeezzed) to fit within the target dimensions while preserving its aspect ratio.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Cheques.AttributeValues.Clip
member.summary: Do not resize the image. Cut off any parts that do not fit within the target dimensions.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.SerialNumber
member.summary: The serial (factory) number of the cash register (KKT) is assigned by the manufacturer at the production stage. This number is unique within the model/batch and may be printed on the KKT housing. It is used for service maintenance, technical support, and matching with the manufacturer's documentation. It remains constant throughout the entire service life of the device, regardless of its registration status with the tax authority.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.RegistrationNumber
member.summary: Сash register system (Kkm) registration Number is assigned at the moment the device is registered with the tax authority. Unlike the factory (serial) number, this number can be changed when the KKT is re-registered.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.SalesCount
member.summary: Sales count.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.NearPaperEnd
member.summary: Refers to the point when the receipt paper roll is nearly finished inside the cash register or receipt printer. It's a proactive warning that tells the cashier: 'Replace the roll now-before it runs out.'

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.FiscalModuleSerialNumber
member.summary: Serial number of the fiscal module used to ensure the secure registration of sales transactions. A fiscal module is a hardware or cloud-based component integrated into a cash register system, designed to cryptographically sign and store transaction data in compliance with fiscal regulations. It helps prevent tampering, ensures data integrity, and enables trusted reporting to tax authorities. The serial number uniquely identifies the module instance and may be required for registration, diagnostics, or compliance audits.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.FiscalModuleActivationDate
member.summary: Activation date of the fiscal storage module. This is the date when the fiscal storage module was first activated or initialized for fiscal use. Typically set during the fiscalization or registration process. This date may be required for compliance, audit, or lifecycle management.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.FiscalModuleExpirationDate
member.summary: Expiration date of the fiscal storage module.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Device.Tasks.CashRegisterStatusField.FiscalModuleHealth
member.summary: Determines the state of the fiscal storage module (the presence of and ).

member.summary: Id of the base order () this kitchen order was created from.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder.IsTopCookingPriority
member.summary: The flag specifies that this order should be cooked out-of-order at kitchen. All orders which have this flag set on will appear above other orders at the kitchen display. Both groups (top priority and non-top priority) will be sorted separately using usual sorting logic (custom cooking priority, then default built-in criterions. Default value is false.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder.CookingPriority
member.summary: Specifies a custom cooking priority. While the application provides built-in prioritization logic for orders based on timing, dish courses, etc., you can override it and enforce custom cooking order based on your external logic. Ideally, all dishes should be cooked timely, but in case of concurrency when kitchen is high-loaded, you have to decide which orders are more important and should be cooked in the first place and which orders are allowed to be delayed. Higher priority values mean that the order, its serve groups and dishes will appear at the kitchen display earlier. Default value is zero. Both positive and negative values are allowed for raising and lowering the priority correspondingly. Orders with the same cooking priority will be sorted using default built-in logic.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder.TabName
member.summary: Optional marker used to specify that the order is a tab. This property contains name of the tab if the order is marked as tab, otherwise null.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder.OriginName
member.summary: Optional marker used to specify order origin. This property contains either null or name of the source order was received from. Specifying origin allows to analyse efficiency of external order sources such as different web-sites, mobile applications and so on.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder.ExternalNumber
member.summary: Optional order number in the external system. This can be used to match the external order to the iiko one. This property contains either null or number of the source order was received from. Specifying origin order number allows to analyse efficiency of external order sources such as different web-sites, mobile applications and so on.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrderCookingItem.IsCookingStarted
member.summary: Whether cooking of the item started. Depending on kitchen settings and waiter intentions item cooking can start immediately after printing or with some delay. Item with short cooking time may wait for long-time cooking item from the same serve group (to be served together), item of second or above course may wait for corresponding serve cheque printing. This property is perfect, expected state, do not mess up with processing status, which means actual status (cook can begin processing before or after the appointed time).

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrderModifierItem.IsSeparate
member.summary: Whether modifier should be cooked separately from its product. In general this property exposes modifier semantics: while some modifiers specify how to cook their product, another modifiers are additional dish parts or even separate dishes. First type completely depends on product and has the same cooking property values, second type is separate and has own kitchen, status etc.

member.summary: On kitchen screens 'By orders' and 'By servings', group dishes by cooking place. By default, dishes are displayed in the same order as on the order editing screen. This setting enables preliminary grouping of dishes by cooking places, within group the order is the same as on the order editing screen, groups themselves are ordered by department name.

member.summary: Show full dish comment on kitchen screen. By default, comment is displayed as single line. This setting enables multi-line comment display.

member.summary: Allowed to transition specific dish to next cooking status by clicking on its name.

member.summary: On kitchen screen, order modifiers by quick input code. By default, modifiers are ordered in the sequence defined in dish modifiers table or scheme. This setting allows explicit ordering for modifiers by non-targeted use of quick input code, including displaying mixed child modifiers from different groups.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IIdentifierCodeHandler
member.summary: Interface for extending the processing and verification of identifier codes (e.g. DataMatrix, GTIN, manually entered codes).
member.remarks: Multiple handlers can be registered and may process the same code. Each handler must provide a unique used to associate verification results and processing metadata.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IIdentifierCodeHandler.SourceKey
member.summary: Unique key identifying this handler. Used to associate verification results and processing flags with the handler.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IIdentifierCodeHandler.Description
member.summary: Human-readable description of the handler.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IIdentifierCodeHandler.CheckIdentifierCodeSupport(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder,Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderProductItem,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Determines whether this handler can process a raw input string representing a product code, and returns detailed support information.
member.param: The order associated with the code.
The product item to which the code is being assigned.
Raw string input representing the product identifier code.
Indicates that the code was obtained while removing the product from the order.
member.returns:
An containing:
— whether this handler can process the code.
Handler-specific flags describing the properties of the code. Must be set if method returns true
— optional descriptive message.
member.remarks: /// This method is called before the identifier code is added to the order, allowing handlers to indicate whether they support and can process the code. If true is returned and the returned include , then will be called during the verification step.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IIdentifierCodeHandler.CheckIdentifierCodeSupport(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder,Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderModifierItem,Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderProductItem,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Determines whether this handler can process a raw input string representing a modifier code, and returns handler-specific processing flags.
member.param: The order associated with the code.
The modifier item to which the code is being assigned.
The product item that the modifier is assigned to.
Raw string input representing the modifier identifier code.
Indicates that the code was obtained while removing the modifier from the order.
member.returns:
An containing:
— whether this handler can process the code.
Handler-specific flags describing the properties of the code. Must be set if method returns true
— optional descriptive message.
member.remarks: This method is called before the identifier code is added to the order, allowing handlers to indicate whether they support processing of the code. If true is returned and the returned include , then will be called during the verification step.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateSchedule
member.summary: A schedule of a service rates.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateSchedule.TimingMode
member.summary: Time rate calculation mode, which specifies how to treat rate schedule interval bounds.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateSchedule.MinimumDuration
member.summary: Minumum duration of a service or null if there is no lower limit. If the lower limit is set and the actual duration is below of limit, it will be rounded up.

list.item: Actual service duration is 15 minutes, is 10 minutes and is not set: service duration will just be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 20 minutes.

Actual service duration is 15 minutes, is 10 minutes and is 1 hour: at first service duration will be rounded up to 1 hour to meet the and then will be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 1 hour.
Actual service duration is 75 minutes, is 10 minutes and is 1 hour: 75 mins already meets the , so it will only be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 1 hour 20 mins.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateSchedule.TimingStep
member.summary: Length of the service duration increment steps. Actual service duration is always rounded up to a multiple of this value.

list.item: Actual service duration is 15 minutes, is 10 minutes and is not set: service duration will just be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 20 minutes.

Actual service duration is 15 minutes, is 10 minutes and is 1 hour: at first service duration will be rounded up to 1 hour to meet the and then will be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 1 hour.
Actual service duration is 75 minutes, is 10 minutes and is 1 hour: 75 mins already meets the , so it will only be rounded up to a multiple of , the result is 1 hour 20 mins.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateSchedule.Items
member.summary: List of non-default rates. Each item is a set of non-intersecting time intervals having own price. All time intervals that are not covered by any of the schedule items have a default price.
member.remarks: Time intervals pricing difference is implemented via binding to different products (). Order item of a service type () has a primary service product () and additional rate products ( ), which are specified by the rates schedule ().

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the .

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateScheduleInterval
member.summary: Describes a time interval within a week, which is used in .

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateScheduleInterval.Day
member.summary: Day of week of the interval.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateScheduleInterval.Start
member.summary: Interval's start point offset relative to start of a day or a service depending on .
member.remarks: For valid values range is [0h; 24h), which means all the day () excluding it's right bound. For valid values range is [0h; 12h), because max service duration is 12h.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.RateScheduleInterval.End
member.summary: Interval's end point offset relative to start of a day or a service depending on .
member.remarks: For valid values range is (0h; 24h], which means all the day () exluding it's left bound. For valid values range is (0h; 12h], because max service duration is 12h. Anyway, is greater than , so that interval has positive length.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the .

member.summary: One or more intervals

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the .

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.ServiceTimingMode
member.summary: Time rate calculation modes, which specifies how to treat rate schedule interval bounds.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.ServiceTimingMode.DayTime
member.summary: Rate schedule interval bounds are relative to a day start. Allows to have different rates for different times of a day, e.g. reduced price for morning hours and advanced price for evening hours, which helps to keep balance between prime time and unpopular periods of a day.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.ServiceTimingMode.ServiceTime
member.summary: Rate schedule interval bounds are relative to a service start. It allows to have different rates for different service hours, e.g. full price for the first hour of the service, 90% price for the second hour and so on, which motivates to increase service duration.

member.summary: Optional marker used to specify custom name of the service item.

member.summary: Default price of the service. Some periods may have own prices, see . All prices are per timing step ().

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.Cost
member.summary: Total cost of the service item, sum of costs of the plus .

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.RemainingLimitCost
member.summary: Predicted cost of the remaining time limit. Zero, if the is not set. If the time limit is set, but it's less than total time of the , remaining time limit is zero and therefore it's cost is also zero. For example, if timing step is one minute, the time limit was set to 1 hour and 20 minutes already passed, there will be one or more periods with total time of 20 minutes and their respective costs, then the cost of remaining 40 minutes will be predicted using schedule. This cost will go down with the lapse of time, because while time of some period increases, the remaining time limit decreases. After 15 minutes total periods time will be 35 mins (20+15) and will represent cost of the remaining 25 mins.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.ResultSum
member.summary: Total sum to be paid, sum with discounts and both included and excluded vat.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.TaxPercent
member.summary: Tax percent or null for not taxed order items.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.TimeLimit
member.summary: Total amount of time after which the service will be stopped or null for default limit (12 hours). Includes all periods.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.IsStarted
member.summary: Whether the service is running now.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.RateSchedule
member.summary: Schedule of the service rates.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItem.Periods
member.summary: Service periods, each period represents some rate. For example, if the schedule has different rates for times before and after 10 o'clock, the service was started at 09:30 and now is 11:00, there will be one period with 30 mins length and another period with 60 mins length, both periods may have different prices and therefore different costs.

member.summary: Product that specifies price of the period. Expected to be of types or .

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItemPeriod.ServiceCustomName
member.summary: Optional marker used to specify custom name of the service period.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItemPeriod.Price
member.summary: Price of the service period per timing step ().

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItemPeriod.Cost
member.summary: Cost of the service period.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderServiceItemPeriod.ElapsedTime
member.summary: Exact total elapsed time of the period, includes all runs of the period. Service may be started, then stopped, then started again, or the schedule may switch the service to another rate and then switch back, so this property will accumulate total active time of the period. This is exact unrounded value. For example, if timing step is 10 mins and this period was active only for 2 mins and 42 seconds, the will be calculated for 10 mins anyway, while this property will have an exact value 00:02:42.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderModifierItem.IsSeparate
member.summary: Whether modifier should be cooked separately from its product. In general this property exposes modifier semantics: while some modifiers specify how to cook their product, another modifiers are additional dish parts or even separate dishes. First type completely depends on product and has the same cooking property values, second type is separate and has own kitchen, status etc.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrderModifierItem.AmountIndependentOfParentAmount
member.summary: Whether amount of the modifier is absolute or relative to parent amount. When modifier amount depends on parent, it means multiplication — specified amount of modifier should be cooked per each parent unit, otherwise it means addition — specified amount of modifier should be cooked for whole parent.

member.summary: Gets estimated guests count, that is greater than or equal to added guests count. When single order is used to serve many people and handling each of them as a separate guest may be inconvenient, this property will indicate estimated guests count while guests collection will contain as many guests as needed for waiters.

member.summary: Optional marker used to specify order origin. This property contains either null or name of the source order was received from. Specifying origin allows to analyse efficiency of external order sources such as different web-sites, mobile applications and so on.

member.summary: Specific data for iikoCard51.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder.WasPrepaid
member.summary: Whether order was prepaid. Any processed or deleted prepay.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder.IsTopCookingPriority
member.summary: The flag specifies that this order should be cooked out-of-order at kitchen. All orders which have this flag set on will appear above other orders at the kitchen display. Both groups (top priority and non-top priority) will be sorted separately using usual sorting logic (custom cooking priority, then default built-in criterions. Default value is false.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder.CookingPriority
member.summary: Specifies a custom cooking priority. While the application provides built-in prioritization logic for orders based on timing, dish courses, etc., you can override it and enforce custom cooking order based on your external logic. Ideally, all dishes should be cooked timely, but in case of concurrency when kitchen is high-loaded, you have to decide which orders are more important and should be cooked in the first place and which orders are allowed to be delayed. Higher priority values mean that the order, its serve groups and dishes will appear at the kitchen display earlier. Default value is zero. Both positive and negative values are allowed for raising and lowering the priority correspondingly. Orders with the same cooking priority will be sorted using default built-in logic.

member.name: P:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder.PriceCategory
member.summary: Price category.

member.summary: Identifier of the delivery that existed before splitting it by multiple cash registers. Depending on settings, a single delivery can be split by multiple cash registers before payment, producing multiple deliveries. At this moment SourceId of all produced deliveries is set to Id of the source delivery. If delivery was not splitted by cash registers, SourceId returns null.

member.summary: Specifies how to select printer to print a section-related document on a specific terminal.
member.remarks: Each has printer settings and each terminal (iikoFront application instance) also may have it's own printer settings, so it's necessary to specify, which settings to use to select a printer.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Print.PrinterSelectionMode.Default
member.summary: Treat section settings as a base level and terminal settings as an override. Printer will be selected by terminal settings if they specify some printer for a document, otherwise printer will be selected using section settings.

member.summary: Select printer using section settings. Terminal settings will be ignored.

member.name: F:Resto.Front.Api.Data.Print.PrinterSelectionMode.ByTerminal
member.summary: Select printer using terminal settings. Currently, section settings will be used as a fallback if printer cannot be selected by terminal, so this option coincides with , but it's just an implementation detail, which may be changed at any time.

Custom payable amount. Leave it null to use default logic or set custom value to override default logic. Payable part of the amount must be between zero (which means that whole modifier is free of charge) and (which means that whole modifier is payable).
Predefined fixed price of the modifier, allows to use external quotation instead of default price list. null means default price.

member.summary: Resets whether latest printed bill is actual to 'false' value. Use when you want delivery bill to be print again according to settings. For example, if you changed courier and wish to have actual name on the bill, call this action and delivery will automatically printed according to settings. On the contrary, if you don't want bill to be printed again (for example, courier is written to the bill later by hands), you have no need to call this action.
member.param: Order to reset whether its bill is considered to be actual.

member.summary: Create order combo item. Order must contain new combo items.

Amount of combo.
Price of combo.
Unique identifier of source combo (template identifier).
Identifier of the loyalty program.
Combo items dictionary. Key - combo group identifier with its name, Value - item id (must be contained in order).

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Exceptions.EntityModifiedException
member.summary: The exception that is thrown when versioned entity was modified by someone between reading an entity and applying changes to it. In general case parallel changes cannot be merged automatically, so optimistic locking prevents overwriting someones changes or getting inconsistent entity state.
member.remarks: Plugin should re-read changed entity, ensure that rejected changes are compatible with new entity state and re-apply them.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.Exceptions.PaymentActionCancelledException
member.summary: Exception to notify that payment action was cancelled.
member.remarks:
Used for cancellation of operation when the reason of cancellation is obvious.
For example, user pressed "Cancel" button during collecting of required data - it's clear that operation will not be resumed.
No message box will be shown.
If the reason is not obvious, use that will show message box for user.

For example, if you changed courier and wish to have actual name on the bill, call this action and delivery will automatically printed according to settings. member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.ILicensingService.AcquireSlot(System.Int32,System.Guid)
member.summary: Acquires one slot from the specified module for client with specified identifier. This method will temporarily bind slot to the client, repeated acquisitions from the same module for the same client will return the same slot.
member.param: The licensable module identifier.
The calling client identifier.
member.returns: A token for single licensing unit (plugin instance or external connection), disposing it releases slot and unbinds the slot from the client.
member.exception: License slot could not be acquired.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.ILicensingService.IsEnabled(System.Int32)
member.summary: Checks whether the specified module is enabled in the license.
member.remarks: Module is enabled when there is an active (not expired) license for this module. This method just checks if the module itself is enabled, it doesn't check whether the module contains any available slots.
member.param: The licensable module identifier.
member.returns: true if the specified module is enabled.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService
member.summary: Provides a set of operations.
member.remarks: There is a global instance of the service which is available

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.RegisterExternalOperation``2(System.String,System.String,System.Func{System.ValueTuple{``0,System.String,System.Int32,System.Guid},``1},System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationBinder,System.Runtime.Serialization.ISurrogateSelector)
member.summary: Exposes a custom operation to be available for call by other plugins. The registration is not permanent, it holds true until disposing the return value, or plugin shutdown.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Identifies the external service, just a namespace for a set of interrelated operations, which helps to group them logically. It's recommended to choose a unique name to avoid collisions with other plugins, i.e. include company's name or plugin's name into the service name. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.

In aggregate with identifies the operation. Both names have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins, so that they will be able to call the operation referring it by service and operation name. The combination of a service name and operation name must be unique at the same time. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.
The operation implementation, which will be called by other plugins via .
A serialization binder for custom mapping between types and names. Default is null.
The to use for serialization. Default is null.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.CallExternalOperation``2(System.Int32,System.String,System.String,``0,Resto.Front.Api.Data.Organization.ITerminal,System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationBinder,System.Runtime.Serialization.ISurrogateSelector)
member.summary: Calls an operation which is implemented in another plugin.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Licensable module identifier of the plugin to be called. That module id have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external service which is implemented in another plugin. That name have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external operation which is implemented in another plugin and have been registered using . That name have to be pre-shared between producer and consumer plugins.
The data which will be transferred as an incoming argument into the external operation.
Terminal for executing the external operation remotely. Leave it null to execute the operation on the local terminal.
A serialization binder for custom mapping between types and names. Default is null.
The to use for serialization. Default is null.
member.returns: Response returned by the producer plugin.
member.exception: When an external operation calling or execution throws any kind of exception, caller receives the with original message and reason. Orignal stacktrace can be found in api.log.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.ExecuteContinuousOperation``1(System.Func{Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService,``0})
member.summary: Executes the callback function as one continuous operation by providing temporary instance of , which holds internal locks taken on lockable entities (such as order) until the operation ends. It's strongly recommended to avoid potentially obstructive calls in the callback, i.e network calls, heavy I/O or CPU-bound calls, thread blocking and so on, because touched entities will remain locked for a long time.
member.remarks: It could be useful for calling several methods of the local instance sequentially when it's important to call them one by one without outside changes. While all committed changes will immediately become visible for everyone, no one except of the provided callback will be allowed to alter entities which were touched via that short-living (input argument of the callback). For example, you can create a new order and add there guests, products and external prepay using one edit session, but you cannot print order items or process external prepay in the same session, because these operations can't be rolled back and thus aren't available via . Executing these operations separately on a global isn't reliable because there is a chance to be interrupted by a user, other plugin or background entity change handlers, which may prevent further our edits by applying conflicting changes or by locking an entity for a long time.
member.param: Callback to be executed continuously.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.GetHostDatabaseId
member.summary: Returns database id for local terminal, where the plugin is currently being executed.
member.remarks: Important to monitor the database id, when reading incrementally data based on revision. The revision grows monotonically only within this database. The database id cannot change while the terminal is running. It's enough to check the database id once, when starting the terminal. If the database id has changed when starting the terminal, you need to read the data starting from revision zero.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.RegisterExternalOperation(System.String,System.String,System.Func{System.ValueTuple{System.Byte[],System.String,System.Int32,System.Guid},System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Exposes a custom operation to be available for call by other plugins. The registration is not permanent, it holds true until disposing the return value, or plugin shutdown.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Identifies the external service, just a namespace for a set of interrelated operations, which helps to group them logically. It's recommended to choose a unique name to avoid collisions with other plugins, i.e. include company's name or plugin's name into the service name. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.

In aggregate with identifies the operation. Both names have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins, so that they will be able to call the operation referring it by service and operation name. The combination of a service name and operation name must be unique at the same time. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.
The operation implementation, which will be called by other plugins via .

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.CallExternalOperation(System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.Byte[],Resto.Front.Api.Data.Organization.ITerminal)
member.summary: Calls an operation which is implemented in another plugin.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Licensable module identifier of the plugin to be called. That module id have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external service which is implemented in another plugin. That name have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external operation which is implemented in another plugin and have been registered using . That name have to be pre-shared between producer and consumer plugins.
The data which will be transferred as an incoming argument into the external operation.
Terminal for executing the external operation remotely. Leave it null to execute the operation on the local terminal.
member.returns: Response returned by the producer plugin.
member.exception: When an external operation calling or execution throws any kind of exception, caller receives the with original message and reason. Orignal stacktrace can be found in api.log.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.GetExternalOperations
member.summary: Returns the list of all currently registered external operations with their service names and owner plugins' licensable module identifiers.

member.summary: Returns iikoTransport settings.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.ExecuteContinuousOperation(System.Action{Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService})
member.summary: Executes the callback function as one continuous operation by providing temporary instance of , which holds internal locks taken on lockable entities (such as order) until the operation ends. It's strongly recommended to avoid potentially obstructive calls in the callback, i.e network calls, heavy I/O or CPU-bound calls, thread blocking and so on, because touched entities will remain locked for a long time.
member.remarks: It could be useful for calling several methods of the local instance sequentially when it's important to call them one by one without outside changes. While all committed changes will immediately become visible for everyone, no one except of the provided callback will be allowed to alter entities which were touched via that short-living (input argument of the callback). For example, you can create a new order and add there guests, products and external prepay using one edit session, but you cannot print order items or process external prepay in the same session, because these operations can't be rolled back and thus aren't available via . Executing these operations separately on a global isn't reliable because there is a chance to be interrupted by a user, other plugin or background entity change handlers, which may prevent further our edits by applying conflicting changes or by locking an entity for a long time.
member.param: Callback to be executed continuously.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.GetOrganizationFingerprint
member.summary: Returns a fingerprint which can be used to identify an iikoRMS server instance.
member.remarks: Sometimes iikoRMS server instances are being cloned with entire database, so that all their key entities have duplicated ids. Fingerprints help to detect changes of some specific server instance properties, which are usually different for cloned instances, but sometimes also may change on the same instance without any cloning. It is assumed that the plugin compares the currently received fingerprint with previously remembered one to ensure that it communicates with the same iikoRMS server instance. Fingerprints equality means that there is the same server instance. When fingerprints are different, plugin should somehow check, whether it is another instance or the fingerprint of the original instance has been changed. The method requires a connection to the iikoRMS server, so calling this method in offline mode will end up with an exception.
member.exception: Underlying network request to the iikoRMS server has failed.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.SetKitchenOrderProcessingStatus(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder,System.Nullable{Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.KitchenOrderProcessingStatus})

member.remarks: By default, the status of a kitchen order is calculated from the statuses of its items. The status assigned by this operation overrides this logic.

member.summary: Changes cooking status of the specified cooking items and modifiers.
member.param: Order which contains the specified cooking items and modifiers.
Cooking items.
Modifiers which are cooked separately from their products. Use to check whether the modifier is cooked separately.
New processing status to be set for all specified cooking items and modifiers.
member.returns: Whether the operation was successful. Kitchen orders use optimistic locking model, so someone could apply conflicting changes to the same cooking items and modifiers just before this method call, in that case plugin should re-read the kitchen order, re-check conditions and call this method again if it still makes sense.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.RegisterCustomRoundingHandler(System.Func{Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder,System.Decimal})
member.summary: Allows to provide custom order sum rounding implementation.
member.remarks: Usually order sum rounding is used to speed up the service and convenience of customers by discarding pennies, or as a legal requirement. For example, if is 1320.74, the callback may return -0.74 to round the sum down to 1320, or return 0.01 to round the sum up to 1320.75 (to the nearest multiple of 0.05).
member.param: The rounding logic implementation, which will be called to calculate order sum. Callback gets an order and returns a sum, by which the result sum of the order should be increased or decreased. In case of zero no rounding will be applied, in case of an exception default rounding will be applied.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService.GetExternalNotificationSubscription(System.String)
member.summary: Get or registers external notification subscription.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external notifications. iikoFront just transfers notification and its data between plugins without knowing any details. Plugin`s external notification registers using and plugin call it using . Any plugin can call notification.
member.param: The name of the notification being registered or the notification being subscribed to.

member.summary: Gets path to the directory where plugin should store it's data.
member.remarks: Contents of that directory will automatically be zipped and deleted in case if iikoFront app has to reset all it's data and start with a clean state. Put here things that is related to iikoFront data.

member.summary: Gets path to the directory where plugin should store it's configuration files (not a data).
member.remarks: Config files will not be deleted during iikoFront app data reset.

member.returns: Plugin settings or null if there is no settings associated with plugin key and entity.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IPluginIntegrationService.GetSupportedApiVersions(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Get a list of API version names, which are supported by the iikoFront application.
member.param: Whether to include prerelease versions. true — all supported API versions will be returned, false — only released API versions will be returned. Released API version works identically on each iikoFront app version () which supports that API version, while prerelease versions are subject to change and may be incompatible between iikoFront app versions.
member.remarks: Can be used by meta-plugins which deal with other plugins.
member.returns: List of supported API versions.

member.name: T:Resto.Front.Api.PluginContext
member.summary: Provides access to api services.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.PluginContext.Initialize(System.IServiceProvider,System.Action,Resto.Front.Api.ILog)
member.summary: Fills the context with Api services on plugin startup. This method isn't intended to be called from plugin.
member.remarks: Primarily this method is used by Api infrastructure, but it also can be used in unit tests for checking plugin's code. When writing tests against code which is using static properties of , you can initialize the context with dummy (mocked) services. Services are retrieved from the provider when corresponding static properties are called, so for testing purposes you may provide minimum set of services which are actually used in the code being tested.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.PluginContext.Uninitialize
member.summary: Resets the context to uninitialized state. This method isn't intended to be called from plugin.
member.remarks: Use this method in unit tests to reset the context and re-initialize with appropriate services for each test fixture.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.PrepareDeliveryForSending(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IDeliveryOrder,Resto.Front.Api.Data.Security.ICredentials,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Prepares delivery for sending by courier, switches from to .
member.remarks: This method may add some items according to the product auto-addition settings. Payment time should be set to on-delivery-closing in the settings.
member.param: The credentials returned by method.
Delivery to prepare for sending.

Whether to stop method execution and throw if order changed according to the product auto-addition settings. Default is false, which means to proceed anyway. Set this flag to true if you have preconditions which should be checked again after adding new items to the delivery. Handle exceptions of type , check preconditions again and call this method again if you are ok with changes.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.GetHostDatabaseId
member.summary: Returns database id for local terminal, where the plugin is currently being executed.
member.remarks: Important to monitor the database id, when reading incrementally data based on revision. The revision grows monotonically only within this database. The database id cannot change while the terminal is running. It's enough to check the database id once, when starting the terminal. If the database id has changed when starting the terminal, you need to read the data starting from revision zero.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.RegisterExternalOperation(System.String,System.String,Resto.Front.Api.RemotingHelpers.RemotableFunc{System.ValueTuple{System.Byte[],System.String,System.Int32,System.Guid},System.Byte[]})
member.summary: Exposes a custom operation to be available for call by other plugins. The registration is not permanent, it holds true until disposing the return value, or plugin shutdown.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Identifies the external service, just a namespace for a set of interrelated operations, which helps to group them logically. It's recommended to choose a unique name to avoid collisions with other plugins, i.e. include company's name or plugin's name into the service name. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.

In aggregate with identifies the operation. Both names have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins, so that they will be able to call the operation referring it by service and operation name. The combination of a service name and operation name must be unique at the same time. Min length is 1, max length is 50 chars.
The operation implementation, which will be called by other plugins via .

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.CallExternalOperation(System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.Byte[],Resto.Front.Api.Data.Organization.ITerminal)
member.summary: Calls an operation which is implemented in another plugin.
member.remarks: Different plugins running under the same iikoFront application instance can communicate via external operations. iikoFront just transfers requests and responses between plugins without knowing any details. Both producer and consumer plugins should understand the data structure, developers should negotiate a protocol specification, pre-share operation names, handle versioning and so on. Producer plugin registers an operation using and consumer plugin call it using .
member.param: Licensable module identifier of the plugin to be called. That module id have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external service which is implemented in another plugin. That name have to be pre-shared with consumer plugins.
Name of the external operation which is implemented in another plugin and have been registered using . That name have to be pre-shared between producer and consumer plugins.
The data which will be transferred as an incoming argument into the external operation.
Terminal for executing the external operation remotely. Leave it null to execute the operation on the local terminal.
member.returns: Response returned by the producer plugin.
member.exception: When an external operation calling or execution throws any kind of exception, caller receives the with original message and reason. Orignal stacktrace can be found in api.log.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.GetExternalOperations

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.ExecuteContinuousOperation(Resto.Front.Api.RemotingHelpers.RemotableAction{Resto.Front.Api.IOperationService})
member.summary: Executes the callback function as one continuous operation by providing temporary instance of , which holds internal locks taken on lockable entities (such as order) until the operation ends. It's strongly recommended to avoid potentially obstructive calls in the callback, i.e network calls, heavy I/O or CPU-bound calls, thread blocking and so on, because touched entities will remain locked for a long time.
member.remarks: It could be useful for calling several methods of the local instance sequentially when it's important to call them one by one without outside changes. While all committed changes will immediately become visible for everyone, no one except of the provided callback will be allowed to alter entities which were touched via that short-living (input argument of the callback). For example, you can create a new order and add there guests, products and external prepay using one edit session, but you cannot print order items or process external prepay in the same session, because these operations can't be rolled back and thus aren't available via . Executing these operations separately on a global isn't reliable because there is a chance to be interrupted by a user, other plugin or background entity change handlers, which may prevent further our edits by applying conflicting changes or by locking an entity for a long time.
member.param: Callback to be executed continuously.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.GetOrganizationFingerprint
member.summary: Returns a fingerprint which can be used to identify an iikoRMS server instance.
member.remarks: Sometimes iikoRMS server instances are being cloned with entire database, so that all their key entities have duplicated ids. Fingerprints help to detect changes of some specific server instance properties, which are usually different for cloned instances, but sometimes also may change on the same instance without any cloning. It is assumed that the plugin compares the currently received fingerprint with previously remembered one to ensure that it communicates with the same iikoRMS server instance. Fingerprints equality means that there is the same server instance. When fingerprints are different, plugin should somehow check, whether it is another instance or the fingerprint of the original instance has been changed. The method requires a connection to the iikoRMS server, so calling this method in offline mode will end up with an exception.
member.exception: Underlying network request to the iikoRMS server has failed.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.SetKitchenOrderProcessingStatus(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.IKitchenOrder,System.Nullable{Resto.Front.Api.Data.Kitchen.KitchenOrderProcessingStatus})
member.summary: Changes cooking status of the kitchen order.
member.remarks: By default, the status of a kitchen order is calculated from the statuses of its items. The status assigned by this operation overrides this logic.
member.param: Order whose processing status needs to be changed.
New processing status to be set for the kitchen order. null if you want to return to calculating order status based on the statuses of its elements.

member.summary: Changes cooking status of the specified cooking items and modifiers.
member.param: Order which contains the specified cooking items and modifiers.
Cooking items.
Modifiers which are cooked separately from their products. Use to check whether the modifier is cooked separately.
New processing status to be set for all specified cooking items and modifiers.
member.returns: Whether the operation was successful. Kitchen orders use optimistic locking model, so someone could apply conflicting changes to the same cooking items and modifiers just before this method call, in that case plugin should re-read the kitchen order, re-check conditions and call this method again if it still makes sense.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.AddStatusBarInfo(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Byte,System.Double)
member.summary: Shows text in iikoFront status bar.
member.param: Text to be shown in iikoFront status bar. Text is limited to 255 symbols, everything over the limit will be cut.
Whether the status bar item describes an error. Error message will be shown in red with an error icon.
Higher rank means created status bar item is to be placed on right side of lesser ranked items, items on right side may be shortened if lack of free space.
Means the size in relation to other status bar items.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.IOperationServiceInternal.RegisterCustomRoundingHandler(Resto.Front.Api.RemotingHelpers.RemotableFunc{Resto.Front.Api.Data.Orders.IOrder,System.Decimal})
member.summary: Allows to provide custom order sum rounding implementation.
member.remarks: Usually order sum rounding is used to speed up the service and convenience of customers by discarding pennies, or as a legal requirement. For example, if is 1320.74, the callback may return -0.74 to round the sum down to 1320, or return 0.01 to round the sum up to 1320.75 (to the nearest multiple of 0.05).
member.param: The rounding logic implementation, which will be called to calculate order sum. Callback gets an order and returns a sum, by which the result sum of the order should be increased or decreased. In case of zero no rounding will be applied, in case of an exception default rounding will be applied.

member.summary: Allows modifying the document before the formatting. Returning 'null' from the callback will result in no changes being applied to the document markup.
member.param: Callback to return modified document.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.OperationArgumentExtensions.TryGetProductMenuImage(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Assortment.IProduct)
member.summary: Returns a byte sequence presenting an image to be shown as a background on a menu button for the specified product. If the product doesn't have an image, null will be returned.
member.remarks: The image may be of any format, depending on what the user has uploaded.
member.param: Product to get menu image for.

member.name: M:Resto.Front.Api.OperationArgumentExtensions.TryGetProductGroupMenuImage(Resto.Front.Api.Data.Assortment.IProductGroup)
member.summary: Returns a byte sequence presenting an image to be shown as a background on a menu button for the specified product group. If the product group doesn't have an image, null will be returned.
member.remarks: The image may be of any format, depending on what the user has uploaded.
member.param: Product group to get menu image for.
